notes: WIN-FND-005. Source-only ledger proving the row's 'compact/comfortable metrics compiled; selectable density is target-only' claim is internally consistent and not silently regressing. It pins the 15 single-profile native metric roles (re-parsed independently of check-material-theme.py), the target compact/comfortable table (docs/design/01-foundations.md section 6), MATERIAL_DESIGN.md's density honesty language, the calc-chrome.json per-surface density carve-out consistency, and a Git-tracked selector-absence guard. It implements no selector and advances none of the row's build/pixel/matrix/perf/compat gates: extending the metric schema, a Tools > Options density surface, and rendered evidence are the separate build gates. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false.

design_honesty.note: MATERIAL_DESIGN.md's Desktop density section must keep both named profiles and its 'implemented and verified separately' honesty language.
markers: must be implemented and verified separately

carveout.note: calc-chrome.json's density carve-out (--tb, --menu) must stay byte-consistent with the section-6 master table (px stripped) and keep status 'specified', so a per-surface value cannot silently diverge from the foundation.

selector_absence.note: A density selection would surface as a .ui widget id or an officecfg property carrying 'density'. A Git-tracked walk over *.ui and *.xcs/*.xcu must find zero such controls; any match means density became selectable and this ledger must be revisited.
